ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡ أُوحِيَ إِلَيَّ أَنَّهُ ٱسۡتَمَعَ نَفَرٞ مِّنَ ٱلۡجِنِّ فَقَالُوٓاْ إِنَّا سَمِعۡنَا قُرۡءَانًا عَجَبٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul oohiya ilayya annahu istamaAAa nafarun mina aljinni faqaloo inna samiAAna quranan AAajaban
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, [O Muhammad], "It has been revealed to me that a group of the jinn listened and said, 'Indeed, we have heard an amazing Qur'an.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “It has been revealed to me that a group of jinn listened ˹to the Quran,˺ and said ˹to their fellow jinn˺: ‘Indeed, we have heard a wondrous recitation.

ayahs.textArabic: يَهۡدِيٓ إِلَى ٱلرُّشۡدِ فَـَٔامَنَّا بِهِۦۖ وَلَن نُّشۡرِكَ بِرَبِّنَآ أَحَدٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yahdee ila alrrushdi faamanna bihi walan nushrika birabbina ahadan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: It guides to the right course, and we have believed in it. And we will never associate with our Lord anyone.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It leads to Right Guidance so we believed in it, and we will never associate anyone with our Lord ˹in worship˺.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَنَّهُۥ تَعَٰلَىٰ جَدُّ رَبِّنَا مَا ٱتَّخَذَ صَٰحِبَةٗ وَلَا وَلَدٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waannahu taAAala jaddu rabbina ma ittakhatha sahibatan wala waladan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [it teaches] that exalted is the nobleness of our Lord; He has not taken a wife or a son
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Now, we believe that˺ our Lord—Exalted is His Majesty—has neither taken a mate nor offspring,

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَنَّهُۥ كَانَ يَقُولُ سَفِيهُنَا عَلَى ٱللَّهِ شَطَطٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waannahu kana yaqoolu safeehuna AAala Allahi shatatan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And that our foolish one has been saying about Allah an excessive transgression.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and that the foolish of us used to utter ˹outrageous˺ falsehoods about Allah.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَنَّا ظَنَنَّآ أَن لَّن تَقُولَ ٱلۡإِنسُ وَٱلۡجِنُّ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waanna thananna an lan taqoola alinsu waaljinnu AAala Allahi kathiban
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And we had thought that mankind and the jinn would never speak about Allah a lie.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We certainly thought that humans and jinn would never speak lies about Allah.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَنَّهُۥ كَانَ رِجَالٞ مِّنَ ٱلۡإِنسِ يَعُوذُونَ بِرِجَالٖ مِّنَ ٱلۡجِنِّ فَزَادُوهُمۡ رَهَقٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waannahu kana rijalun mina alinsi yaAAoothoona birijalin mina aljinni fazadoohum rahaqan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And there were men from mankind who sought refuge in men from the jinn, so they [only] increased them in burden.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And some men used to seek refuge with some jinn—so they increased each other in wickedness.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَنَّهُمۡ ظَنُّواْ كَمَا ظَنَنتُمۡ أَن لَّن يَبۡعَثَ ٱللَّهُ أَحَدٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waannahum thannoo kama thanantum an lan yabAAatha Allahu ahadan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they had thought, as you thought, that Allah would never send anyone [as a messenger].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And those ˹humans˺ thought, just like you ˹jinn˺, that Allah would not resurrect anyone ˹for judgment˺.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَنَّا لَمَسۡنَا ٱلسَّمَآءَ فَوَجَدۡنَٰهَا مُلِئَتۡ حَرَسٗا شَدِيدٗا وَشُهُبٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waanna lamasna alssamaa fawajadnaha muliat harasan shadeedan washuhuban
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And we have sought [to reach] the heaven but found it filled with powerful guards and burning flames.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Earlier˺ we tried to reach heaven ˹for news˺, only to find it filled with stern guards and shooting stars.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَنَّا كُنَّا نَقۡعُدُ مِنۡهَا مَقَٰعِدَ لِلسَّمۡعِۖ فَمَن يَسۡتَمِعِ ٱلۡأٓنَ يَجِدۡ لَهُۥ شِهَابٗا رَّصَدٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waanna kunna naqAAudu minha maqaAAida lilssamAAi faman yastamiAAi alana yajid lahu shihaban rasadan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And we used to sit therein in positions for hearing, but whoever listens now will find a burning flame lying in wait for him.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We used to take up positions there for eavesdropping, but whoever dares eavesdrop now will find a flare lying in wait for them.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَنَّا لَا نَدۡرِيٓ أَشَرٌّ أُرِيدَ بِمَن فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ أَمۡ أَرَادَ بِهِمۡ رَبُّهُمۡ رَشَدٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waanna la nadree asharrun oreeda biman fee alardi am arada bihim rabbuhum rashadan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And we do not know [therefore] whether evil is intended for those on earth or whether their Lord intends for them a right course.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Now, we do not really know whether evil is intended for those on earth, or their Lord intends for them what is right.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَنَّا مِنَّا ٱلصَّٰلِحُونَ وَمِنَّا دُونَ ذَٰلِكَۖ كُنَّا طَرَآئِقَ قِدَدٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waanna minna alssalihoona waminna doona thalika kunna taraiqa qidadan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And among us are the righteous, and among us are [others] not so; we were [of] divided ways.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Among us are those who are righteous and those who are less so. We have been of different factions.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَنَّا ظَنَنَّآ أَن لَّن نُّعۡجِزَ ٱللَّهَ فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَلَن نُّعۡجِزَهُۥ هَرَبٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wanna thananna an lan nuAAjiza Allaha fee alardi walan nuAAjizahu haraban
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And we have become certain that we will never cause failure to Allah upon earth, nor can we escape Him by flight.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Now,˺ we truly know that we cannot frustrate Allah on earth, nor can we escape from Him ˹into heaven˺.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَنَّا لَمَّا سَمِعۡنَا ٱلۡهُدَىٰٓ ءَامَنَّا بِهِۦۖ فَمَن يُؤۡمِنۢ بِرَبِّهِۦ فَلَا يَخَافُ بَخۡسٗا وَلَا رَهَقٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waanna lamma samiAAna alhuda amanna bihi faman yumin birabbihi fala yakhafu bakhsan wala rahaqan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when we heard the guidance, we believed in it. And whoever believes in his Lord will not fear deprivation or burden.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: When we heard the guidance ˹of the Quran˺, we ˹readily˺ believed in it. For whoever believes in their Lord will have no fear of being denied ˹a reward˺ or wronged.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَنَّا مِنَّا ٱلۡمُسۡلِمُونَ وَمِنَّا ٱلۡقَٰسِطُونَۖ فَمَنۡ أَسۡلَمَ فَأُوْلَٰٓئِكَ تَحَرَّوۡاْ رَشَدٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waanna minna almuslimoona waminna alqasitoona faman aslama faolaika taharraw rashadan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And among us are Muslims [in submission to Allah], and among us are the unjust. And whoever has become Muslim - those have sought out the right course.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And among us are those who have submitted ˹to Allah˺ and those who are deviant. So ˹as for˺ those who submitted, it is they who have attained Right Guidance.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَمَّا ٱلۡقَٰسِطُونَ فَكَانُواْ لِجَهَنَّمَ حَطَبٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waama alqasitoona fakanoo lijahannama hataban
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But as for the unjust, they will be, for Hell, firewood.'
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And as for the deviant, they will be fuel for Hell.’”

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَلَّوِ ٱسۡتَقَٰمُواْ عَلَى ٱلطَّرِيقَةِ لَأَسۡقَيۡنَٰهُم مَّآءً غَدَقٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallawi istaqamoo AAala alttareeqati laasqaynahum maan ghadaqan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [Allah revealed] that if they had remained straight on the way, We would have given them abundant provision
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Had the deniers followed the Right Way, We would have certainly granted them abundant rain to drink—

ayahs.textArabic: لِّنَفۡتِنَهُمۡ فِيهِۚ وَمَن يُعۡرِضۡ عَن ذِكۡرِ رَبِّهِۦ يَسۡلُكۡهُ عَذَابٗا صَعَدٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Linaftinahum feehi waman yuAArid AAan thikri rabbihi yaslukhu AAathaban saAAadan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So We might test them therein. And whoever turns away from the remembrance of his Lord He will put into arduous punishment.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: as a test for them. And whoever turns away from the remembrance of their Lord will be admitted by Him into an overwhelming punishment.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَنَّ ٱلۡمَسَٰجِدَ لِلَّهِ فَلَا تَدۡعُواْ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ أَحَدٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waanna almasajida lillahi fala tadAAoo maAAa Allahi ahadan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [He revealed] that the masjids are for Allah, so do not invoke with Allah anyone.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The places of worship are ˹only˺ for Allah, so do not invoke anyone besides Him.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَنَّهُۥ لَمَّا قَامَ عَبۡدُ ٱللَّهِ يَدۡعُوهُ كَادُواْ يَكُونُونَ عَلَيۡهِ لِبَدٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waannahu lamma qama AAabdu Allahi yadAAoohu kadoo yakoonoona AAalayhi libadan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And that when the Servant of Allah stood up supplicating Him, they almost became about him a compacted mass."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Yet when the servant of Allah stood up calling upon Him ˹alone˺, the pagans almost swarmed over him.

ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡ إِنَّمَآ أَدۡعُواْ رَبِّي وَلَآ أُشۡرِكُ بِهِۦٓ أَحَدٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul innama adAAoo rabbee wala oshriku bihi ahadan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, [O Muhammad], "I only invoke my Lord and do not associate with Him anyone."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “I call only upon my Lord, associating none with Him ˹in worship˺.”

ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡ إِنِّي لَآ أَمۡلِكُ لَكُمۡ ضَرّٗا وَلَا رَشَدٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul innee la amliku lakum darran wala rashadan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, "Indeed, I do not possess for you [the power of] harm or right direction."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Say, “It is not in my power to harm or benefit you.”

ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡ إِنِّي لَن يُجِيرَنِي مِنَ ٱللَّهِ أَحَدٞ وَلَنۡ أَجِدَ مِن دُونِهِۦ مُلۡتَحَدًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul innee lan yujeeranee mina Allahi ahadun walan ajida min doonihi multahadan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, "Indeed, there will never protect me from Allah anyone [if I should disobey], nor will I find in other than Him a refuge.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Say, “No one can protect me from Allah ˹if I were to disobey Him˺, nor can I find any refuge other than Him.

ayahs.textArabic: إِلَّا بَلَٰغٗا مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَرِسَٰلَٰتِهِۦۚ وَمَن يَعۡصِ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ فَإِنَّ لَهُۥ نَارَ جَهَنَّمَ خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهَآ أَبَدًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Illa balaghan mina Allahi warisalatihi waman yaAAsi Allaha warasoolahu fainna lahu nara jahannama khalideena feeha abadan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But [I have for you] only notification from Allah, and His messages." And whoever disobeys Allah and His Messenger - then indeed, for him is the fire of Hell; they will abide therein forever.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹My duty is˺ only to convey ˹the truth˺ from Allah and ˹deliver˺ His messages.” And whoever disobeys Allah and His Messenger will certainly be in the Fire of Hell, to stay there for ever and ever.

ayahs.textArabic: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا رَأَوۡاْ مَا يُوعَدُونَ فَسَيَعۡلَمُونَ مَنۡ أَضۡعَفُ نَاصِرٗا وَأَقَلُّ عَدَدٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hatta itha raaw ma yooAAadoona fasayaAAlamoona man adAAafu nasiran waaqallu AAadadan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [The disbelievers continue] until, when they see that which they are promised, then they will know who is weaker in helpers and less in number.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Only when they see what they have been threatened with will they know who is weaker in helpers and inferior in manpower.

ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡ إِنۡ أَدۡرِيٓ أَقَرِيبٞ مَّا تُوعَدُونَ أَمۡ يَجۡعَلُ لَهُۥ رَبِّيٓ أَمَدًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul in adree aqareebun ma tooAAadoona am yajAAalu lahu rabbee amadan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, "I do not know if what you are promised is near or if my Lord will grant for it a [long] period."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Say, “I do not know if what you are promised is near or my Lord has set a distant time for it.

ayahs.textArabic: عَٰلِمُ ٱلۡغَيۡبِ فَلَا يُظۡهِرُ عَلَىٰ غَيۡبِهِۦٓ أَحَدًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: AAalimu alghaybi fala yuthhiru AAala ghaybihi ahadan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [He is] Knower of the unseen, and He does not disclose His [knowledge of the] unseen to anyone
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹He is the˺ Knower of the unseen, disclosing none of it to anyone,

ayahs.textArabic: إِلَّا مَنِ ٱرۡتَضَىٰ مِن رَّسُولٖ فَإِنَّهُۥ يَسۡلُكُ مِنۢ بَيۡنِ يَدَيۡهِ وَمِنۡ خَلۡفِهِۦ رَصَدٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Illa mani irtada min rasoolin fainnahu yasluku min bayni yadayhi wamin khalfihi rasadan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Except whom He has approved of messengers, and indeed, He sends before each messenger and behind him observers
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: except messengers of His choice. Then He appoints angel-guards before and behind them

ayahs.textArabic: لِّيَعۡلَمَ أَن قَدۡ أَبۡلَغُواْ رِسَٰلَٰتِ رَبِّهِمۡ وَأَحَاطَ بِمَا لَدَيۡهِمۡ وَأَحۡصَىٰ كُلَّ شَيۡءٍ عَدَدَۢا
ayahs.textTransliteration: LiyaAAlama an qad ablaghoo risalati rabbihim waahata bima ladayhim waahsa kulla shayin AAadadan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That he may know that they have conveyed the messages of their Lord; and He has encompassed whatever is with them and has enumerated all things in number.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: to ensure that the messengers fully deliver the messages of their Lord—though He ˹already˺ knows all about them, and keeps account of everything.”
